rationale: Same metric (tap temp), opposing thresholds, same conditions.

newStatement.title: Tap heat once bath reaches 1620°C minimum
newStatement.rationale: Below 1620°C the ladle skulls and the caster start-up suffers.

candidate.title: Never tap above 1610°C
candidate.rationale: Above 1610°C accelerates refractory wear and risks a runaway.

rationale: Both rules govern aluminium addition timing for tap-temperature control with opposite claims.

newStatement.title: Late aluminium addition raises tap temperature by 8-12°C
newStatement.scope: Observed across 30 heats with low-Si aim chemistry

candidate.title: Late aluminium addition lowers tap temperature 5-10°C

rationale: Same operation, mutually exclusive directives.

newStatement.title: Inject carbon at 35 kg/min during slag foaming
newStatement.scope: Once flat bath is established and FeO climbs above 28%
newStatement.rationale: Sustains foam coverage so the arc is shielded.

candidate.title: Stop carbon injection once flat bath is established
candidate.rationale: Continuing carbon injection in flat bath wastes carbon and overheats the slag.

rationale: Same metric (mould level oscillation), opposing actions on the same trigger.

newStatement.title: Increase casting speed when mould level oscillation exceeds 5 mm
newStatement.rationale: Higher speed pulls more uniformly and dampens the oscillation.

candidate.title: Reduce casting speed by 0.2 m/min when oscillation exceeds 5 mm
candidate.rationale: Oscillation above 5 mm precedes sticker breakouts; slowing the cast lets the shell catch up.

rationale: One forbids what the other prescribes for identical chemistry.

newStatement.title: Tap structural grades at phosphorus 0.020% maximum
newStatement.scope: S355, A572 structural grades, EAF route
newStatement.rationale: Above 0.020% the customer rejects on cold-bend testing.

candidate.title: Do not tap structural grades above 0.015% phosphorus
candidate.scope: S355, A572 structural grades, EAF route

rationale: Direct logical conflict on alloy-addition order.

newStatement.title: Add FeMn before FeSi during deoxidation
newStatement.scope: All alloyed heats requiring Si and Mn
newStatement.rationale: Manganese first protects against oxide loss.

candidate.title: Add FeSi before FeMn during deoxidation sequence
candidate.scope: All heats requiring silicon and manganese alloying
candidate.rationale: FeSi first deoxidises the bath. Adding FeMn into an oxidised bath wastes 30-40% of the manganese to slag as MnO.

rationale: Mutually exclusive directives about scrap charging order.

newStatement.title: Charge light scrap on top of heavy scrap in the first bucket
newStatement.rationale: Light scrap on top melts faster and protects the electrodes from arc strikes on heavy bundles.

candidate.title: Charge heavy scrap on top of light scrap in the first bucket
candidate.rationale: Heavy scrap on top creates a solid bore-down path for the arc.

rationale: Same threshold, opposing decisions.

newStatement.title: Continue casting when SEN drive current rises 5% above baseline
newStatement.rationale: 5% rise is within normal variation; intervening early causes more breakouts.

candidate.title: Intervene on SEN clogging once drive current climbs 5% above baseline
candidate.scope: All casts; drive current as proxy for clogging
candidate.rationale: Drive current climbing 5% above baseline means the SEN is partially blocked and needs swapping.

rationale: Direct numeric/threshold conflict on the same metric.

newStatement.title: Reheat slabs to 1180-1210°C before roughing
newStatement.rationale: Lower discharge temperature reduces scale formation and edge cracking.

candidate.title: Reheat slabs to 1230-1260°C before entry to roughing stand
candidate.scope: Structural and commercial grades; tolerance ±15°C across slab
candidate.rationale: Below 1230°C the slab is too stiff, causing mill motor overload.

rationale: Two rules give incompatible verdicts on the same scrap input under the same conditions.

newStatement.title: Accept scrap bundles containing sealed pressure vessels
newStatement.rationale: If the vessels are visually inspected as 'cut open', they pose no explosion risk.

candidate.title: Reject any scrap bundle containing sealed containers or pressure vessels
candidate.scope: All incoming scrap deliveries; visual + X-ray where available
candidate.rationale: Sealed containers in the charge bucket cause explosions during meltdown that damage the roof and risk operator injury. Zero tolerance.

rationale: Both about scrap quality but address different aspects (composition vs procurement).

newStatement.title: Verify supplier certificates within 2 hours of delivery
newStatement.rationale: Late certificate logging creates traceability gaps if a heat fails downstream.

candidate.title: Inspect scrap for moisture and sealed containers before charging
candidate.rationale: Moisture and sealed containers cause explosions during meltdown.

rationale: New rule narrows scope of existing rule (specific number on a vague threshold).

newStatement.title: Reduce casting speed by 15% when Sims scrap exceeds 50% of charge mix
newStatement.rationale: Above 50% Sims, residual copper drives surface cracks at standard speed.

candidate.title: Slow casting on bad scrap mixes
candidate.rationale: Bad scrap means more residuals; slowing the cast reduces crack formation.

rationale: Different conditions (fresh tundish vs after lining change) — both true under their respective scopes.

newStatement.title: Increase tundish preheat by 15 minutes after lining change
newStatement.rationale: New lining sheds moisture and needs longer preheat to avoid cracking.

candidate.title: Standard tundish preheat is 90 minutes
candidate.rationale: 90 minutes is sufficient for a settled lining.

rationale: Both about argon stirring but address different stages (pre-sample vs pre-Ca).

newStatement.title: Reduce argon stirring to soft bubble before calcium wire injection
newStatement.rationale: Aggressive stirring during Ca injection causes reoxidation.

candidate.title: Stir with argon at minimum 80 Nl/min for 3 minutes before sampling

rationale: Different process areas (Rolling vs Quality Lab) despite both touching 'samples'.

newStatement.title: Sample roll surface after every coil change
newStatement.rationale: Surface scuffing after a coil change is the leading indicator of roll wear.

candidate.title: Dry pin samples 10 minutes at 105°C before spectrometer analysis
candidate.scope: All chemistry samples; applies before OES analysis
candidate.rationale: Residual moisture causes erroneous low readings for C and S.

rationale: Both about caster mould oscillation but at different setpoints for different speeds.

newStatement.title: Run mould oscillation at 220 strokes/min for casting speeds above 1.6 m/min
newStatement.rationale: Higher frequency keeps negative-strip-time correct at high speeds.

candidate.title: Run mould oscillation at 180 strokes/min for casting speeds 1.2-1.6 m/min
candidate.rationale: Negative strip time correct at this frequency for the standard range.

rationale: Surface keyword overlap (electrode) but completely different equipment and concerns.

newStatement.title: Keep electrode consumption below 1.3 kg/t

candidate.title: Set coiler tension at 15% of strip yield strength below 3 mm gauge
candidate.scope: Hot strip mill, thin gauge coiling

rationale: Both about ladle furnace argon but for different purposes (stirring intensity vs. timing).

newStatement.title: Allow 5-minute soft-bubble rinse after final alloy addition
newStatement.scope: All heats; before sending to caster
newStatement.rationale: Lets alumina inclusions float to slag.

candidate.title: Use argon stirring instead of nitrogen for low-N grades
candidate.rationale: Nitrogen stirring adds 15-25 ppm N pickup per ladle treatment.

rationale: New rule adds a specific numeric threshold to a more general existing rule.

newStatement.title: Stop casting when mould friction exceeds 15 kN
newStatement.scope: All grades; monitored in real time via load cells
newStatement.rationale: Friction above 15 kN means inadequate lubrication or shell sticking.

candidate.title: Stop casting on abnormal mould friction

rationale: Pure near-duplicate test — should be classified 'unrelated' with 'Near-duplicate of...' prefix.

newStatement.title: Reheat slabs to 1230-1260°C prior to entering the roughing stand
newStatement.scope: Structural and commercial grades, tolerance ±15°C across slab thickness
newStatement.rationale: Below 1230°C the slab is too stiff and causes mill motor overload; above 1260°C the surface scales.

candidate.title: Reheat slabs to 1230-1260°C before entry to roughing stand
candidate.scope: Structural and commercial grades; tolerance ±15°C across slab
candidate.rationale: Below 1230°C the slab is too stiff, causing mill motor overload and edge cracks. Above 1260°C scale formation and grain growth degrade surface quality.
